_comment: ROOT-OWNED, TAMPER-PROOF Claude Code policy for the `claude` agent. Deployed by claude-harness.nix to /etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json — the HIGHEST settings tier; the claude user cannot override or edit it. IMPORTANT — where safety actually comes from: NOT this allow list. The real boundary is (1) the OS: the agent is a non-root user (uid 1002, no wheel/docker) whose only privileged path is the tiny sudoers allowlist in sudo.nix; (2) this file's `deny` list + the root-owned PreToolUse guard (pretooluse-guard.sh), which hard-deny destructive commands no matter how phrased; (3) the PR-merge gate: nothing the agent does is APPLIED to the system until Chris merges to main and comin rebuilds. Given those three, `allow` is intentionally broad (Bash(*)) so the agent can work in its own box without a prompt on every read/diagnostic command. Tighten by adding to `deny`/the guard, NOT by narrowing `allow`. See ./README.md and ./pretooluse-guard.sh.
_comment_lockdown: allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly: user/project allow|ask|deny rules are IGNORED — only this file's rules apply (the agent can't self-grant OR self-narrow outside a reviewed PR). allowManagedHooksOnly: only this file's hook runs (the agent can't inject an auto-allow PreToolUse hook). permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode='disable': --dangerously-skip-permissions / bypassPermissions mode is rejected at startup. Doc-verified: code.claude.com/docs permissions.md + permission-modes.md.
